title: EITT Entropy-Invariance Test on Chemical Mixture Compositions
author: Peter Higgins, Rogue Wave Audio
purpose: Test whether Shannon entropy of chemical mixture compositions exhibits near-invariance under geometric-mean temporal decimation, extending EITT from energy/financial domains into chemistry where hard physical constraints (mass balance, stoichiometry, phase rules) govern the simplex.

hypothesis.H1: Shannon entropy of mixture mole-fraction compositions is near-invariant (|delta_M| < 2%) under geometric-mean block decimation along a condition axis (temperature or concentration path), provided the system is near-stationary.
hypothesis.H0_null: Entropy drifts significantly (|delta_M| > 5%) because chemical constraint geometry differs fundamentally from temporal autocorrelation in energy systems.
hypothesis.boundary_hypothesis: Phase boundaries, miscibility gaps, and reaction discontinuities will break EITT — these are the chemistry equivalent of non-stationarity.

data_sources.name: CheMixHub
data_sources.description: Standardised benchmark for chemical mixture property prediction. ~500k data points, 7 public datasets, all compositions as mole fractions.
data_sources.suitability: EXCELLENT — compositions already in mole fractions (simplex-native), multiple domains, temperature-varying conditions, CSV format.

datasets.name: Miscible Solvents — Density
datasets.constraint: mole fractions sum to 1, miscibility constraint

datasets.name: ILThermo — Viscosity
datasets.components: binary ionic liquid mixtures, 699 distinct molecules
datasets.axis: temperature at fixed composition, or composition at fixed T
datasets.constraint: mole fractions sum to 1, Arrhenius-type T dependence

data_sources.name: ChemKED-database
data_sources.description: YAML-format combustion kinetics experiments. Fuel/oxidizer/diluent mole fractions with ignition delay times at various T, P.
data_sources.suitability: MODERATE — compositions are simplex-native (mole fractions of fuel mixtures), but data is per-experiment not time-series. Need to construct pseudo-time axis from T or P sweeps.
data_sources.notes: Good for testing EITT on stoichiometrically constrained systems. PyKED Python package available for parsing.

data_sources.name: InterMat (NIST)
data_sources.description: DFT interface calculations for material heterostructures. Atomic coordinates and lattice parameters.
data_sources.suitability: POOR — not naturally compositional data. Crystal structures, not mixture compositions. Would need heavy reframing to map to CoDa.
data_sources.notes: Reject for EITT testing. Retain as reference for future atomic-scale CoDa work if someone converts site-occupancy data to compositions.

primary_experiment.why: Largest single dataset (75,992 points), binary mixtures with clear T-sweep axes, hard physical constraints, mole fractions pre-computed.

protocol.step_1: Clone chemixhub repo, load ILThermo viscosity processed_data.csv
protocol.step_2: For each unique mixture (fixed component pair), extract mole-fraction composition vector and sort by temperature (ascending) to create a pseudo-time series
protocol.step_3: Filter to mixtures with >= 10 data points along T axis (sufficient for decimation)
protocol.step_4: Apply CLR transform to mole-fraction compositions
protocol.step_5: Compute Shannon entropy H(x) at each temperature point
protocol.step_6: Apply geometric-mean block decimation at M = 2, 3, 5 (replace blocks of M consecutive compositions with their geometric mean, re-close)
protocol.step_7: Compute delta_M = [mean H(decimated) - mean H(original)] / mean H(original) × 100%
protocol.step_9: Compare with Aitchison variance and TV distance under same decimation
protocol.step_10: Identify boundary cases: mixtures near phase transitions, miscibility gaps

expected_results.if_EITT_holds: |delta_M| < 2% for well-behaved mixtures along smooth T-sweeps. Aitchison distance expands. TV distance changes. Shannon entropy near-invariant.
expected_results.if_EITT_fails: |delta_M| > 5% — chemical constraint geometry fundamentally different from temporal autocorrelation. This is a valuable boundary finding.
expected_results.boundary_cases: Phase transitions, precipitation events, miscibility gaps should break EITT. Document these as the chemistry equivalent of non-stationarity (solar fast/nuclear break in energy domain).

secondary_experiments.description: Binary/ternary solvent mixtures. Test EITT along composition sweep at fixed T (mole fraction of component A from 0→1). This is a composition-axis decimation rather than T-axis.
secondary_experiments.interest: Does EITT hold when the 'time' axis is actually composition ratio? This tests whether the invariance is about the simplex geometry itself.

secondary_experiments.description: Drug in mixed solvents. Composition sweep with hard solubility boundary.
secondary_experiments.interest: Natural phase boundary test. Solubility limit = the point where EITT should break. Can we detect the boundary from entropy drift?

secondary_experiments.description: Polymer-salt compositions with ionic conductivity. Percolation threshold.
secondary_experiments.interest: Another phase-like boundary. Does EITT break at the percolation threshold? Does K_eff detect the transition?

secondary_experiments.description: Fuel/oxidizer/diluent mole fractions. Group by fuel type, sweep T or P.
secondary_experiments.interest: Stoichiometric constraints are the hardest chemical constraint. If EITT holds here, the simplex geometry argument is very strong.

coda_mapping.composition: Mole fractions of mixture components → point on the simplex S^D
coda_mapping.closure: Mole fractions sum to 1 by definition (physical constraint, not arbitrary normalisation)
coda_mapping.perturbation: Changing mixture ratios = perturbation in Aitchison geometry
coda_mapping.time_axis: Temperature sweep, pressure sweep, or composition sweep (pseudo-time)
coda_mapping.decimation: Geometric-mean block averaging of neighbouring compositions along axis

constraints.mass_balance: Total mass conserved — atoms are neither created nor destroyed
constraints.stoichiometry: Reaction networks conserve atoms per element
constraints.phase_rule: Gibbs phase rule F = C - P + 2 limits degrees of freedom
constraints.solubility: Phase boundaries impose hard limits on composition space

what_this_proves_if_it_works: EITT is not a quirk of energy-market autocorrelation. The near-invariance of Shannon entropy under geometric-mean decimation is a property of the Aitchison geometry on the simplex itself, observable in any domain where compositions evolve smoothly under physical constraints. Chemistry provides the hardest test because the constraints are fundamental laws of nature, not market conventions.
what_this_proves_if_it_fails: EITT requires temporal autocorrelation (persistence), not just compositional structure. Chemical systems that jump across phase boundaries or undergo discontinuous reactions violate the smoothness assumption. This maps precisely to the energy-domain finding that solar (fast) and nuclear (discrete event) break EITT. The failure mode tells us exactly what EITT needs to work.
honest_disclosures: Temperature sweep is a pseudo-time axis, not true temporal data. Results must be interpreted accordingly.
Binary mixtures (D=2) live on S^1, which is trivially 1-dimensional. Ternary and higher are more interesting for CoDa.
CheMixHub data is curated for ML benchmarking, not specifically for CoDa analysis. Some preprocessing choices may affect compositional structure.
ILThermo data quality varies by publication source. Outlier filtering needed.
This is exploratory. We are testing whether EITT extends to chemistry, not claiming it does.
governance: HUF-GOV. Measure, report, file. If it works, report. If it fails, report the failure mode. No intervention on the data.
